mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive 60-day returns when both series expand, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery regime reflects supply chain normalization and capacity utilization gains, which directly boost corporate profit margins and yield positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 20-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the mechanism predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, triggering immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 60% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand that translates into higher corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of industrial production contraction signal immediate supply-side weakness and impending earnings downgrades, causing rapid risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 50% of cases following two consecutive INDPRO declines, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below random chance in backtesting.
